title: Citroen Dispatch Crew Cab
body:
[![Citroen Dispatch Crew Cab](../images/image-3-e1745079468615-600x382.webp)](https://www.vansales.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/image-3-e1745079468615.webp)

Citroen Dispatch Crew Cab The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab stands out with its impressive,172kg payload and.0m³ load volume. This van emerges as a smart choice for businesses that…

that need to transport both teams and equipment.  
The van’s strong.0-litre Blue HDi engine generateshp andNm of torque. This versatile vehicle blends power with efficiency and achieves.7mpg in combined fuel economy. The complete tech package has a-inch touchscreen that integrates Apple CarPlay and Android Auto. Advanced driver assistance systems keep the crew safe during daily operations.

I tested this crew van extensively in ground business scenarios for six months. Now I can tell you if this £31,640 investment delivers on its promises. Let me share what you should know about the new Citroen Despatch before its March launch.

## Citroen Despatch Crew Cab Van (2025): Specs and Setup for Business Use

The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab comes in two practical body lengths that strike a perfect balance between passenger transport and cargo space. The M variant gives you.2m² of load space with akg payload. The longer XL version boosts this to.0m² capacity with a slightly lowerkg payload. Both models share the samemm load width andmm load height, which means consistent usability no matter which version you choose.

Under the hood sits a strong.0-litre BlueHDi engine paired with a six-speed manual transmission. This powerhouse deliversbhp andNm of torque, making it perfect for demanding business needs. The engine’s performance doesn’t compromise efficiency – it achieves up to.7mpg in combined cycles.

The Crew Van’s interior is designed with comfort in mind. It fits six passengers easily, and the second row features three individually contoured seats that come with armrests and under-seat storage. You still get up tom³ of cargo volume behind the second row – perfect for teams that need to transport both people and equipment.

The Moduwork feature turns this van into a mobile office. This smart system has a load-through bulkhead and folding passenger seat that stretches the maximum load length tomm in M models andmm in XL variants. Twin sliding side doors measuringmm wide bymm high make access a breeze.

The van’s technology package impresses with a-inch touchscreen infotainment system. It has DAB radio, Bluetooth connectivity, and wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto integration. Enterprise trim models come with rear parking sensors, automatic lights and wipers. The Driver version steps up safety with blind spot monitoring and flankguard detection.

Starting at £31,640 for the Enterprise M model, the Despatch Crew Cab is a significant investment. Notwithstanding that, its detailed specification has front electric windows, cruise control, air conditioning, and multipleV sockets throughout the cabin and cargo area. This makes it a complete solution for businesses of all types.

## Months of Daily Use: How It Performs for Business Transport

My six months of daily business use with the Citroen Despatch Crew Cab has taught me more about its ground capabilities than any brochure could show.

The Despatch handles town driving with surprising agility. Its light and precise steering makes tight parking spots substantially easier to manage. The van’s excellent forward visibility and large door mirrors help you navigate busy streets well, though it lacks the lower blind spot mirrors you’ll find on the Transit Custom.

The manual gearbox shifts smoothly in stop-start traffic. I mostly drove with loads that took care of the natural bounce in the ride. The van cruises effortlessly on motorways. The quiet cabin keeps wind and engine noise out, so you can chat or listen to the radio easily.

The BlueHDi S&S-speed Manual promises.7 mpg on paper. My ground tests over six months showed between.5-34.4 mpg, varying with load and trip type. These numbers are good for a van of this size.

Having dual sliding side doors is a great advantage over competitors that offer just one. These doors, plus the-degree opening rear ones, made loading much quicker. The load-through bulkhead proved useful countless times – I could slide longer items under the front passenger seat.

I boosted the van’s organisation by adding modular shelving on the driver’s side. This created compartments that changed how I stored and accessed my tools. The setup kept the floor clear while adding more storage space.

The Despatch Crew Cab might not match the Transit Custom’s smooth ride or handling. Yet it shines where it counts – giving you a perfect mix of passenger comfort and load space in a package that makes daily business runs feel easy.

## Is It Worth the Price? Value Analysis for Fleet Buyers

Price plays a significant role in fleet purchase decisions. The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab Enterprise’s M variant with BlueHDi engine starts at £31,030, making it a substantial investment for businesses that need versatile transport solutions.

The Despatch crew cab’s competitive positioning offers compelling value. It costs about £5,000 less than both Ford Transit Custom and Mercedes Vito, giving immediate upfront savings without compromising capability. The similar price point with sister brands Peugeot, Fiat, Vauxhall and Toyota creates interesting choices. Toyota’s extendable-year warranty programme could benefit long-term fleet operators significantly.

Service intervals of,000 miles or two years reduce maintenance disruption for busy fleets. The standard warranty package gives three years or,000 miles of mechanical coverage, plus paintwork (2 years) and anti-perforation (5 years) protection. Businesses worried about maintenance budgets can opt for Citroen’s Service Plans with monthly payment options that spread costs predictably.

The range maintains respectable fuel economy. The BlueHDi S&S-speed manual achieves up to.2mpg on the WLTP cycle, though ground testing showed.5-34.4mpg depending on load and type of experience. Urban-focused operations might find the electric e-Despatch appealing with ranges up to miles offering long-term savings potential.

Fleet buyers looking at the secondhand market will benefit from depreciation. This-2019 Despatch generation holds impressive residual values – models with,000-75,000 miles currently sell for around £9,000. Three-year used examples provide excellent value for expanding fleets.

The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab delivers solid value by balancing purchase price, running costs, standard equipment levels, and practical versatility. Fleet managers get a detailed standard specification including a-inch touchscreen, rear parking sensors, automatic lights and wipers without facing extensive options lists for business essentials.

## Conclusion

The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab has proven itself as a capable workhorse after six months of testing. This van strikes a good balance between practical versatility and running costs. Real-world fuel economy ranges from.5-34.4 mpg, which works well for daily business operations, even though it doesn’t match the official figures.

The van’s £31,030 base price just needs careful thought, but it makes sense when you look at the details. You’ll save £5,000 compared to Ford and Mercedes alternatives, plus it comes with detailed standard features. Fleet managers will appreciate the built-in safety tech, six-person seating comfort, and the smart Moduwork system without paying extra for optional add-ons.

This van might not have all the premium touches of its expensive rivals, but it shines where it counts. The Despatch handles daily operations reliably and offers practical load space with reasonable running costs. Businesses that need both crew and cargo transport will find the,172kg payload capacity,.0m³ load volume, and true six-person comfort an attractive combination.

My extended time with the latest Despatch shows it’s a strong player in the crew van market. The mix of capability, comfort and value makes it a solid choice for businesses looking for versatile crew transport, despite its few shortcomings.

## FAQs

**Q1. What is the payload capacity of the Citroen Despatch Crew Cab (2025)?** The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab (2025) offers a substantial payload capacity of,172kg, making it suitable for businesses that need to transport both personnel and equipment.

**Q2. How many passengers can the Citroen Despatch Crew Cab accommodate?** The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab is designed to comfortably seat six passengers, with three individually contoured seats in the second row that include armrests and under-seat storage.

**Q3. What is the fuel economy of the Citroen Despatch Crew Cab in real-world conditions?** In real-world conditions, the Citroen Despatch Crew Cab achieves between.5-34.4 mpg, depending on the load and type of journey. This is lower than the official figure of.7 mpg but still reasonable for its class.

**Q4. How does the Citroen Despatch Crew Cab compare in price to its competitors?** The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab is priced approximately £5,000 less than comparable models from Ford and Mercedes, making it an attractive option for cost-conscious fleet buyers.

**Q5. What technology features are included in the Citroen Despatch Crew Cab?** The Citroen Despatch Crew Cab comes equipped with a-inch touchscreen infotainment system, DAB radio, Bluetooth connectivity, and wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto integration. It also includes practical features like rear parking sensors, automatic lights, and wipers.
